# @zakkster/lite-headless/skeleton

Loading-state coordinator. Manages a `loading -> ready` state machine for one
or more independent data sources, optionally gating the reveal with a
`minVisibleMs` threshold to prevent flash. The consumer provides placeholder
elements (with their own shimmer animation) and content elements; the
primitive paints `data-loading` + `aria-busy` so consumer CSS can swap which is
visible.

## Quick example — single state

```js
import { createSkeleton } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/skeleton";

const sk = createSkeleton({
    minVisibleMs: 200,   // prevent flash if fetch resolves super-fast
});

sk.attachRoot(rootEl);
sk.attachPlaceholder(placeholderEl);
sk.attachContent(contentEl);

// On data arrival:
const data = await fetchUser();
renderUserInto(contentEl, data);
sk.reveal();
```

```html
<div class="profile-card">
    <div class="placeholder" data-skeleton>
        <div class="shimmer-circle"></div>
        <div class="shimmer-line wide"></div>
        <div class="shimmer-line short"></div>
    </div>
    <div class="content" data-skeleton-content>
        <!-- populated when data arrives -->
    </div>
</div>
```

```css
[data-skeleton-root][data-loading] [data-skeleton-content] {
    opacity: 0; visibility: hidden;
}
[data-skeleton-root][:not([data-loading])] [data-skeleton] {
    display: none;
}
[data-skeleton-content] {
    transition: opacity 250ms;
}
[data-skeleton] {
    background: linear-gradient(90deg, #1a1c20 0%, #2a2d33 50%, #1a1c20 100%);
    background-size: 200% 100%;
    animation: skeleton-shimmer 1.4s ease-in-out infinite;
    border-radius: 4px;
}
@keyframes skeleton-shimmer {
    0%   { background-position: 200% 0; }
    100% { background-position: -200% 0; }
}
```

## Quick example — multi-source coordination

When the page has several independent data sources (user profile, recent
posts, follower count), you want all placeholders to reveal at once when
every source is ready. Declare the sources up front; `resolve()` them as
they arrive; the primitive flips to ready when the last one resolves.

```js
const sk = createSkeleton({
    sources: ["user", "posts", "followers"],
    minVisibleMs: 200,
});

sk.attachRoot(rootEl);
// ... attach placeholders + contents ...

fetchUser().then(d => { renderUser(d);   sk.resolve("user"); });
fetchPosts().then(d => { renderPosts(d); sk.resolve("posts"); });
fetchFollowers().then(d => { renderFollowers(d); sk.resolve("followers"); });
// When all three have called resolve(), sk.reveal() fires automatically.
```

`resolve()` on an undeclared source auto-registers it (so dynamic data sources
work too). The all-resolved auto-reveal is only triggered when `sources.size > 0`
in the internal map; calling `resolve("foo")` on a freshly-constructed skeleton
with no declared sources will register and auto-reveal immediately, which is
the right behavior for "one-shot" loads.

## Options

| Option            | Default      | Notes                                                  |
| ----------------- | ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `sources`         | `[]`         | Array of source names for multi-source coordination    |
| `minVisibleMs`    | `0`          | Minimum time placeholders stay visible after construction; reveal is deferred until this elapses |
| `initiallyReady`  | `false`      | Start in `ready` state (e.g., SSR-prehydrated content) |
| `onReveal`        | -            | Fires once on each `loading -> ready` edge             |
| `onConceal`       | -            | Fires once on each `ready -> loading` edge             |

## ARIA

| Element       | Painted attributes                                                       |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Root          | `role="status"` · `aria-live="polite"` · `aria-busy="true\|false"` · `data-skeleton-root` · `data-X="loading\|ready"` |
| Placeholder   | `data-skeleton` · `data-loading` · `aria-hidden="true"` (when ready)       |
| Content       | `data-skeleton-content` · `data-loading` · `aria-hidden="true"` (when loading) |

`role="status"` + `aria-live="polite"` on the root means a screen reader will
announce the content the moment the skeleton flips to ready. The polite
liveness lets the announcement queue behind any in-progress utterance instead
of interrupting it.

## State machine

```
                      construct
                          │
                          ▼
                  ┌──────────────┐
       conceal()  │   loading    │
       reset()    │ aria-busy=t  │  ◀───────────┐
       ◀──────────┤              │              │
                  └──────┬───────┘              │
                         │                      │
                         │  setReady(true)      │
                         │  or all resolved     │
                         │  (after minVisible)  │
                         ▼                      │
                  ┌──────────────┐              │
                  │    ready     │              │
                  │ aria-busy=f  ├──────────────┘
                  │              │   conceal()
                  └──────────────┘   reset()
```

## API

```js
sk.ready()                  // reactive boolean accessor
sk.pendingSources()         // reactive: string[] of unresolved sources
sk.isResolved(name)         // O(1) per-source predicate

sk.setReady(true)           // reveal (respects minVisibleMs)
sk.setReady(false)          // conceal (immediate; cancels pending timer)
sk.reveal()                 // = setReady(true)
sk.conceal()                // = setReady(false); also resets sources
sk.resolve(source)          // mark a source resolved
sk.reset()                  // mark all sources unresolved + setReady(false)

sk.attachRoot(el)           // -> off() cleanup
sk.attachPlaceholder(el)
sk.attachContent(el)

sk.destroy()
```

`attach*` returns an `off()` cleanup function that removes the painted
attributes. Useful when the same skeleton drives multiple cards and you want
to detach one without destroying the whole instance.

## Common patterns

### Fade in content, fade out placeholders

```css
[data-skeleton-content] { opacity: 0; transition: opacity 200ms; }
[data-skeleton-root][:not([data-loading])] [data-skeleton-content] { opacity: 1; }
[data-skeleton] { transition: opacity 150ms; }
[data-skeleton-root][:not([data-loading])] [data-skeleton] {
    opacity: 0; pointer-events: none;
}
/* After the fade, remove from layout: */
[data-skeleton-root][:not([data-loading])] [data-skeleton] { display: none; }
```

### Skeleton inside a virtual table (loading rows)

```js
const tableSk = createSkeleton({ minVisibleMs: 250 });
tableSk.attachRoot(tableContainer);

// Render fake placeholder rows while loading:
for (const placeholderRow of placeholderRows) {
    tableSk.attachPlaceholder(placeholderRow);
}
tableSk.attachContent(realTableEl);

fetch("/rows").then(rows => {
    mountTable(realTableEl, rows);
    tableSk.reveal();
});
```

### Re-loading on filter change

```js
filterInput.addEventListener("input", () => {
    sk.reset();   // back to loading
    fetchFiltered(filterInput.value).then(rows => {
        renderRows(rows);
        sk.reveal();
    });
});
```

### Paired with a progress primitive for "loading > N%" UX

```js
const pg = createProgress({ value: 0, max: totalBytes });
const sk = createSkeleton({ minVisibleMs: 200 });

pg.attachRoot(progressEl);
sk.attachRoot(skeletonRoot);
sk.attachPlaceholder(placeholderEl);   // shows the spinning bar + skeleton
sk.attachContent(contentEl);

stream.on("byte", n => pg.setValue(n));
stream.on("end", () => sk.reveal());   // progress will be at 100% by then
```

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-skeleton sources="user posts" min-visible-ms="200">
    <div data-skeleton class="placeholder-card">...</div>
    <div data-skeleton-content class="real-card">...</div>
</lite-skeleton>
```

```js
const sk = document.querySelector("lite-skeleton");
sk.resolve("user");
sk.resolve("posts");
// → fires CustomEvent "reveal" on the host
```

### Reactive attributes

| Attribute        | Effect                                            |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `sources`        | Space-separated source names (read once on attach)|
| `min-visible-ms` | Number; default 0 (read once on attach)           |
| `ready`          | Flag attribute -- setting/removing toggles state  |

### Imperative API + events

```js
host.setReady(true)
host.reveal()
host.conceal()
host.resolve("name")
host.reset()
host.ready           // boolean accessor
host.pendingSources  // string[] accessor

host.addEventListener("reveal", () => ...);
host.addEventListener("conceal", () => ...);
```

## When NOT to use

- **A single inline spinner with no content swap** — a CSS-only spinner
  is lighter. Use this primitive only when you have placeholder shapes
  that need to coordinate with real content visibility.
- **Per-row loading indicators in a list** — those are better handled by
  the consumer's render loop directly; the skeleton is for "whole panel"
  loading shells.
- **Optimistic UI mutations** — when you're mutating something the user
  already sees, you don't want to revert to skeleton. Use a smaller
  "saving..." badge or progress primitive instead.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
